text_blocks.text: following infection by activation of B and T cells.
Active immunity can also be generated artificially,
through vaccination. The principle behind vaccination
(also called immunization) is to introduce an antigen
from a pathogen in order to stimulate the immune
system and develop specific immunity against that
associated with that organism. This deliberate
induction of an immune response is successful
because it exploits the natural specificity of the
immune system, as well as its inductibility. With
infectious disease remaining one of the leading
causes of death in the human population,
vaccination represents the most effective manipulation
of the immune system mankind has developed.
